overview: A five-color control-midrange deck built around protecting Avatar Aang while using Airbender's Reversal for flexible interaction and Toph for board stabilization. It aims to out-value opponents through superior card quality and protection, winning against aggressive strategies via disruption and against control/combo through resilient threats and late-game inevitability.
win_conditions: Avatar Aang as the primary win condition, supported by Toph's protection and Airbender's Reversal to bounce blockers or protect Aang from removal.
Chain value engines to out-resource opponents.

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 3-4 lands (prioritize basic lands or duals that enter untapped), at least one early interaction (Airbender's Reversal) or mana accelerator.
mulligan_criteria: Drop hands with 0-1 lands, 4+ lands with no spells, or no way to develop mana by turn 2.

early_game: Turn 1: Play a land, optionally cast a cheap mana rock or cantrip.
Turn 2: Develop into three mana, play Toph or a low-cost threat, and use Airbender's Reversal to bounce an opponent's early threat or protect your own.
Sequence lands to fix colors while preserving untapped lands for protection spells.
mid_game: Five-color decks live and die by mana fixing
prioritize fetching/shocking to maintain flexibility without stalling.
Hoard interaction until you identify the primary threat, and avoid overcommitting to the board if Avatar Aang is vulnerable to sweepers or targeted removal.
Commit threats when you have answered the opponent's removal or have protection ready.
Hold back if they have sweepers, counterspells, or unanswered value engines.
Use Toph to lock down key permanents or protect your board from removal.
late_game: Avatar Aang as the primary win condition, supported by Toph's protection and Airbender's Reversal to bounce blockers or protect Aang from removal.
Chain value engines to out-resource opponents.
Go all-in when you control the board, have protection for Aang, and can swing through remaining blockers.
Commit when the opponent has no sweepers or counterspells in hand.

sequencing_tips: Airbender's Reversal is your most versatile tool—use it to bounce threats, protect Aang, or trigger ETB/untap effects.
Toph likely provides static protection or board lock
Watch for format-specific rules regarding legendary permanents and commander tax if applicable.

matchups.advice: Cast spells in response to their counters, use cheap/cheap-to-cast threats, or split casting costs. Use Airbender's Reversal to bounce their counterspell or protect your own key spells.
Apply early pressure, use discard or artifact destruction, and prioritize card advantage to out-value their acceleration. Don't let them stabilize into a massive board.
Stabilize with early removal and counterspells, use Toph to protect your board, and transition to a control game plan before they close out the game.
Apply consistent pressure, use card draw to out-resource them, and protect Avatar Aang from targeted removal. Go wide or go big depending on their board state.
Win through superior card quality, targeted removal, and protection spells. Out-value them in the late game by protecting your key pieces while disrupting theirs.

common_threats: Watch for mana pools held until end step (counters/wipes), lands played out of sequence (ramp), and graveyard activity (recursion).
Key tells include discarding specific cards, playing multiple lands in a row, or targeting your creatures with spot removal.
